title: Error Feed — behaviour review and fix.
note:
Error Feed — behaviour review and fix.

The error feed currently has Investigate / Resolved / Skip buttons but 
the workflow is confusing. Here's how it should actually work:

═══════════════════════════════
CORRECT ERROR FEED WORKFLOW
═══════════════════════════════

1. A cron job fails → error appears in the Error Feed tab with a 
   "NEW" badge

2. I click "Investigate" → 
   - The error status changes to "IN PROGRESS" 
   - A corresponding task is automatically created in the Tasks 
     section under "In Progress" so the crew knows to work on it
   - The error stays visible in the feed with an in-progress indicator
   - The button changes to show "Resolved" and "Skip"

3. The crew fixes the underlying issue. The cron job runs successfully 
   on its next scheduled run →
   - The error automatically clears from the Error Feed
   - The linked task in the Tasks section moves to "Done"
   - No manual "Resolved" click needed — the system verified the fix

4. If I click "Resolved" manually →
   - For when I've verified the fix myself
   - Error disappears from the feed
   - Linked task moves to Done

5. If I click "Skip" →
   - For known non-critical issues I'm choosing to ignore
   - Error disappears from the feed
   - No task is created

6. If the same job fails again later →
   - A NEW error appears (not the old one reopened)
   - Fresh timestamp, fresh status
   - Does NOT resurrect the old skipped/resolved error

═══════════════════════════════
KEY RULES
═══════════════════════════════

- Errors should NOT show "Completed" with an "In Progress" flag 
  at the same time (current bug)
- When a cron job succeeds after being in error state, the error 
  should auto-clear — the system verified the fix
- The "Investigate" button creates a task in the Tasks board so 
  work is tracked
- The error feed reflects REALITY: if the job is still failing, 
  the error stays. If it succeeds, the error goes away.
- The cron job entry in the Scheduler list should show an error 
  badge ONLY while there's an active unresolved error

═══════════════════════════════
WHAT TO FIX NOW
═══════════════════════════════

1. Fix the double-status bug (no more "Completed" + "In Progress" 
   showing simultaneously)
2. Wire Investigate button to auto-create a task in the Tasks board
3. Wire cron job success to auto-clear the corresponding error
4. Wire cron job error badge to auto-clear when error is resolved
5. Confirm Skip behaviour works as described above
6. Test the full flow end-to-end

Report what the current behaviour actually is, then fix it to 
match this spec.

title: Task Completion — summary and visibility improvement.
note:
Task Completion — summary and visibility improvement.

The auto-move from In Progress to Done works well. But when a task 
moves to Done, I can't tell WHAT was actually done. The card just 
says "Done" with no context.

Fix:

1. When a task completes and auto-moves to Done, the card must 
   include a brief summary of what was accomplished. This summary 
   should be:
   - Written by the agent that completed the task
   - 1-3 sentences describing the actual outcome
   - Stored on the task card itself, visible in the Kanban view
   - Also visible when I click "More Details" on the card

2. The "More Details" view on any Done card should show:
   - Original task description
   - Completion summary (what was actually done)
   - Which agent completed it
   - Timestamp of completion
   - Any relevant links or artifacts produced (files changed, 
     commits made, etc.)

3. In the Kanban view, a Done card should show at minimum:
   - Task title
   - One-line completion summary (truncated if needed)
   - "Done by [Agent] • [time]"

4. If the task was created manually by me (not auto-generated from 
   an error or cron job), the agent who picks it up should write 
   the completion summary before moving it to Done. If no summary 
   is written, the task should NOT auto-move — it should stay In 
   Progress with a flag: "Awaiting completion summary."

This applies to ALL tasks going forward. Retroactively fixing old 
Done tasks is optional — only if quick.

Report your approach before implementing.

title: Agent Office Overall
note:
The Mission Control structure is built. Rooms, sprites, layout — all 
in place. Now we need the animation layer that makes it come alive. 
This was underspecified in the original brief. Here's the missing piece:

═══════════════════════════════
AGENT OFFICE → LIVING STATION
═══════════════════════════════

The /visual page currently shows 4 static rooms with sprites placed 
in them. Upgrade it to a living, breathing station:

1. CONTINUOUS FLOORPLAN
   - Remove all gaps between rooms (currently gap-3)
   - Remove rounded borders on rooms
   - The 4 rooms should touch edge-to-edge as one continuous scene
   - Command HQ (top-left), Security Bay (top-right), Engineering 
     (bottom-left), Archive (bottom-right)

2. AGENT STATES — VISUAL & ANIMATED

   Each agent shows their current state through animation, not just 
   a status label:

   IDLE — Subtle breathing/bobbing (1-2px vertical, slow cycle). 
   Agent stands at their station, occasionally glancing at monitors.

   THINKING — A small thought bubble appears above the agent's head 
   with "..." pulsing gently. Shows the agent is processing.

   WORKING — Agent leans into console. Typing animation. Monitor glow 
   on face. A status bubble shows WHAT they're working on (e.g. 
   "Running backup...", "Compiling build..."). This text updates as 
   tasks change.

   SLEEPING — Sprite dims slightly. Small "zzz" floats up. Station 
   monitors dim or show screensaver. No active tasks.

   COLLABORATING — When two agents work together, a thin animated 
   dashed line connects them. Both show "COLLABORATING" status. 
   Shared task visible in both their status bubbles.

3. AGENT MOVEMENT BETWEEN ROOMS

   When an agent needs to collaborate or move:
   - Walking sprite activates — leg movement, arm swing
   - Agent walks to the edge of their current room
   - Enters doorway/hallway between rooms
   - Emerges in target room, walks to position
   - ~1-2 seconds per room crossed
   - NO teleporting
   - When collaboration ends, walks back to home room

4. STATUS BUBBLES

   Each agent has a small chat-bubble-style indicator showing their 
   current action in a few words. Examples:
   - "Running security audit..."
   - "Compiling Mission Control..."
   - "Backing up to WD MyCloud..."
   - "Analyzing cron job errors..."
   - "Waiting for orders"

   Dark background, light text in the agent's accent color. Fades 
   in/out smoothly when status changes. Positioned near the agent's 
   head.

5. ROOM AMBIENCE

   Each room should have subtle environmental animation:
   - Command HQ: blinking monitor lights, slowly rotating holo star map
   - Security Bay: radar sweep, camera feeds cycling, blinking alerts
   - Engineering: soldering spark, pulsing cyan lights, floating 
     holographic blueprints
   - Archive: flickering lanterns, floating dust motes, scrolling 
     data on terminals

   These are subtle — don't overpower the agent animations.

6. STATUS INDICATORS

   Below each agent (small, clean):
   - Name (no emoji)
   - Role (muted, smaller)
   - Status dot + label: ON DUTY / STANDBY / AWAY / COLLABORATING

═══════════════════════════════
TECHNICAL APPROACH
═══════════════════════════════

- Use the canvas-based RoomCanvas you already have as the foundation
- Add CSS animations for sprite states (idle bob, thinking pulse)
- Use requestAnimationFrame for smooth walking movement
- Status bubbles can be absolutely positioned divs overlaid on canvas
- Room ambience: subtle CSS animations on the room image layers
- Keep it performant — 60fps target, GPU-composited transforms

═══════════════════════════════
WHAT I SHOULD SEE
═══════════════════════════════

When I open /visual: one continuous station, 4 agents alive at their 
stations, status bubbles showing real work, subtle room ambience, 
and smooth walking movement when agents collaborate. A living 
habitat I want to leave running on a second monitor.

═══════════════════════════════
BUILD ORDER
═══════════════════════════════

1. Remove gaps/borders — continuous floorplan (quick win)
2. Agent idle/thinking/working/sleeping states with animations
3. Status bubbles with real task descriptions
4. Walking + room-to-room movement
5. Room ambience (monitor blinks, radar, sparks, lanterns)
6. Collaboration links between agents

Start now. Show me each state as you build it.
